fedEvents.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
fedEvents.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

fedEvents.title: Trump administration expands list of graduate degrees subject to higher borrowing limits
fedEvents.content: Many graduate students will be subject to higher federal student loan caps than previously expected, after a court ruling last week. Here's what to know.

fedEvents.title: Cleveland Fed President Hammack says AI could fuel inflation, rate hikes may be necessary
fedEvents.content: "We've got inflation that's too high, and it's been too high for the past five years," Beth Hammack told CNBC's Sara Eisen.

items.title: Your aging parent has a new romance. Here’s how to protect your inheritance — without looking greedy.
items.content: Before your single mom or dad remarries, talk with them about estate-planning issues.

items.title: This threat to the chip rally has surged to its highest level since 2015
items.content: Volatility threatens top gainers including AMD and Micron.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs were supposed to make selloffs less painful. That theory is being put to the test.
items.content: Crypto investors had hoped that increased institutional adoption and a crypto-friendly administration could help the largest cryptocurrency avoid the painful cycles it once experienced.

items.title: Nike earnings crushed Wall Street’s estimates — but here’s the catch
items.content: Nike’s profit and gross margins were buoyed by a tariff refund.

items.title: ‘She is retired’: Do I dip into my 401(k) to pay my mother’s $30,000 credit-card debt?
items.content: “I want her to live on her Social Security instead of using it to pay off her credit-card debt.”

items.title: Trump discloses expanding financial empire, including Apple stock, celebration coins and crypto
items.content: The president’s new annual filing is nearly four times as lengthy as last year’s disclosure and shows crypto income in the hundreds of millions

items.title: If LeBron James goes to the Warriors, that team’s roster will be stacked with some of the most expensive players in NBA history
items.content: LeBron James’s agent has told the Los Angeles Lakers that he intends to sign with a new team, according to a new report.

items.title: Investors piled into ETFs at a record pace in the first half of 2026. Here’s where their money is flowing.
items.content: Investors have poured money into exchange-traded funds at a rapid pace in the first half of 2026, demonstrating an unrelenting appetite for stocks associated with the AI theme.

items.title: Trump’s energy secretary says global warming is ‘no big deal.’ Meanwhile, a heat emergency is striking the U.S.
items.content: Government scientists warn people to stay indoors this weekend as temperatures in many areas could reach triple digits

items.title: Will the Comcast-NBCU spinoff pay off for investors? Here’s what history has to say.
items.content: Comcast says splitting its cable and broadband business from NBCUniversal will unlock value for both businesses — but media spinoffs historically have resulted in mixed outcomes.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.content: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.content: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.content: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
items.content: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

items.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
items.content: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

items.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
items.content: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

items.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
items.content: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

items.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
items.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

items.title: Natural-Gas Firm Diversified Energy Strikes Deal for Permian Basin Player
items.content: Maverick Natural Resources has operations in Texas and Oklahoma.

items.title: What's New This Tax Season That Can Save You Money
items.content: There are some new wrinkles, especially for those who sell things online or bought an EV.

items.title: Chinese Investors With Few Options Turn to Dividends
items.content: Companies with good payouts are now the hottest bet in China markets.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Fall But Direction for Long-End Yields Still Seen Upward
items.content: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield fell and ING said the the long end of the Treasury curve will continue trading at higher yields even as Trump hasn’t delivered anything to shock markets so far.

items.title: Russia's Crude Exports Fairly Stable Despite U.S. Sanctions, Goldman Says
items.content: Oil edged lower in early trade as Goldman Sachs said the latest round of U.S. sanctions against Russia haven’t had a significant impact on the country’s oil exports.

items.title: The Extra Reward for Owning Stocks Over Bonds Has Disappeared
items.content: There is little sign of crimped demand for equities among individual investors, who remain bullish after two years of blockbuster gains.

items.title: What Will It Take for Home Buyers to Start Asking for a Disaster Discount?

items.title: In China, a Cat-and-Mouse Game to Rein In Crypto
items.content: Recent court cases reveal how middlemen are facilitating a booming trade, eluding a crackdown.

items.title: One House, Three Owners: The Ballooning Cost of the American Dream
items.content: The story of home affordability in the U.S. told from a single front porch.

items.title: Trump Adds Fuel to Claims That Big Banks Discriminate Against Conservatives
items.content: The president has told Bank of America and JPMorgan Chase to stop cutting conservatives off from doing business.

items.content: The LinkedIn co-founder is starting Manas AI with “The Emperor of All Maladies” author Siddhartha Mukherjee.

items.title: Can an App Replace a Personal Trainer? I Tested Nearly a Dozen to Find Out
items.content: Apps can add flexibility and freshness to your routine. Just try not to get injured.

items.content: The billionaire spoke with The Wall Street Journal ahead of his coming book, “Source Code.”

items.title: Canada to Review Amazon Contracts After Quebec Layoffs
items.content: Canada has warned Amazon.com that it is reviewing business ties with the company’s cloud-computing unit following Amazon’s decision to shut down its warehouses in Quebec, leading to the layoff of 1,700 workers.

items.title: TikTok Restoring Service for U.S. Users, After Trump Signals He Will Save It

items.title: How People Actually Make a Living on TikTok
items.content: The platform has provided a surge in discovery and revenue for companies that may not be able to replicate it elsewhere.

items.title: What Happens Now That TikTok's Gone Dark?
items.content: The company told users the app isn’t available and told them to stay tuned. Trump said he will likely intervene.

items.title: How a Little-Known Finnish Company Became One of the World's Hottest Gadget Startups
items.content: Oura’s smart ring measures heart health and can predict when you may be getting sick

items.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
items.content: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

items.title: Iran says it is selling oil at 20% premium as end of U.S. blockade sees 40 million barrels exported
items.content: The ceasefire prompted a surge in crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, where traffic had largely ground to a halt during the conflict.

items.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.content: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

items.title: Nike results top estimates even as China sales drop 12%; retailer expects $986 million tariff refund
items.content: Nike was expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: AI demand fuels investors' portfolios while oil posts biggest monthly decline
items.content: The tech space continues to be fueled by rising artificial intelligence demand, with investors piling their portfolios in the technology, while Amazon Web Services dabbles with Forward Deployed Engineering units to better compete with  OpenAI and Anthropic.

items.title: Record chip rally adds $2 trillion in combined value to Micron, Intel and AMD in second quarter
items.content: Wall Street poured into chipmakers not named Nvidia in the second quarter, as the artificial intelligence boom expanded to include more suppliers.

items.title: Palo Alto, CrowdStrike both have best quarter ever as AI threats bolster cyber demand
items.content: The cybersecurity leaders are leaning into identity security, a critical cyber segment, as AI agents outnumber humans

items.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, blocks Trump order
items.content: President Donald Trump attended oral arguments in the case, underscoring his staunch opposition to granting automatic citizenship to many immigrants' babies.

items.title: Do you really, really love your job? Then you're not alone, according to surprising results from this survey
items.content: Those surveyed showed a 78.9% rate of workers who "reported feeling positive at the end of their shifts."

items.title: Trump's massive defense budget, depleted war machine, spark U.S. state battle for business and jobs
items.content: Trump's huge defense budget request and a race to replenish weapons stocks while building hypersonic missiles lead to war between U.S. states for jobs.

items.title: Jim Cramer says the AI trade has shifted — and these stocks are leading now
items.content: CNBC's Jim Cramer said Wall Street is rewarding the companies supplying the artificial intelligence boom rather than the technology giants funding it.

items.title: Michael Burry says he's shorting Caterpillar for the first time after it nearly doubled in the AI-driven rally of 2026
items.content: "Caterpillar jumped out at me," Burry said. "I have never shorted Caterpillar. It has always done great for me on the long side in the past."

items.title: China-linked actors target more than technology as AI competition with U.S. intensifies
items.content: Cyberattacks from China-based entities are on the rise and not just targeting specific tech, analysts warn, as the U.S. AI race intensifies.

items.title: Trump says Council of Economic Advisers head Pierre Yared is leaving
items.content: The Council of Economic Advisers has provided the president with information and advice on both domestic and international economic policy since 1946.

items.title: Anthropic launches AI drug discovery program, joining tech giants in betting on healthcare
items.content: Anthropic will start an internal drug discovery program, part of a new push to sell artificial intelligence tools to drugmakers.

items.title: Big egg producers settle price inflation probe with DOJ for 53 million eggs — and $3.3M
items.content: Cal-Maine Foods, Versova and Hickman's Egg Ranch agreed to settle allegations of price manipulation by the Department of Justice and 17 states.

items.title: AWS puts $1 billion into new AI unit to embed engineers with customers, joining growing wave
items.content: AWS FDEs will look to leave behind self-sufficient teams with new AI solutions and capabilities in a matter of weeks, the company said.

items.title: After a soft Nike quarter, we have a big decision to make on the stock
items.content: The results are unlikely to improve Nike's standing in the portfolio

items.title: American Airlines brings grab-and-go lounge to New York's JFK
items.content: American Airlines is opening a grab-and-go lounge at New York's JFK

items.title: Private equity in youth sports draws bipartisan scrutiny in Congress
items.content: Democratic and Republican members of Congress expressed alarm at the trend of private equity investment in youth sports.

items.title: FDA lets Philip Morris market Zyn nicotine pouches as less harmful than cigarettes
items.content: The Trump administration has expanded access to nicotine products despite concerns from public health critics about youth use and addiction.

items.title: Why Asia still struggles to create its own mega-IPOs like SpaceX
items.content: Asia has no shortage of entrepreneurs, engineers or giant domestic markets. Yet when it comes to producing the kind of blockbuster listings seen in the U.S., the region continues to lag.

items.title: Social Security gender gap means women receive about $4,800 less in annual benefits. What to know before claiming
items.content: Women tend to earn less and spend more time outside of the workforce, which may reduce their Social Security benefits. Here's what to consider before claiming.

items.title: GOP Rep. Tom Kean says depression diagnosis caused months-long absence from Congress
items.content: New Jersey Rep. Tom Kean said he was diagnosed with depression, explaining a months-long absence from Congress in a competitive district in the midterm elections.

items.title: Blue Origin pivots to redesigned launchpad after explosion in push to fly by end of 2026
items.content: Blue Origin won't be "rebuilding the same pad," and will instead adopt a configuration that was in development for a larger variant of its New Glenn rocket.

items.title: Trump administration expands list of graduate degrees subject to higher borrowing limits
items.content: Many graduate students will be subject to higher federal student loan caps than previously expected, after a court ruling last week. Here's what to know.

items.title: Supreme Court strikes down limits on political parties' campaign spending, in win for GOP
items.content: President Donald Trump took a victory lap after the ruling, writing on Truth Social that it was "A BIG WIN FOR REPUBLICANS."

items.title: Cleveland Fed President Hammack says AI could fuel inflation, rate hikes may be necessary
items.content: "We've got inflation that's too high, and it's been too high for the past five years," Beth Hammack told CNBC's Sara Eisen.

items.title: Small-cap stocks enjoy their best first half in 35 years. Here's what's driving it
items.content: The advance marks a sharp turnaround after years of underperformance versus large-cap peers.

items.title: Brent posts biggest monthly decline since March 2020 as traders monitor U.S.-Iran talks
items.content: The moves come as oil traders monitor the prospect for U.S.-Iran talks in Doha on Tuesday.
